q: What is NNRG?
a: Nalla Narasimha Reddy Education Society's Group of Institutions (NNRG) is a premier multi-disciplinary college established in 2009, affiliated to JNTU Hyderabad and approved by AICTE.

q: When was NNRG established?
a: NNRG was established in 2009.

q: What is NNRG's vision?
a: To be a premier institution ensuring globally competent and ethically strong professionals.

q: What is NNRG's mission?
a: To provide higher education by refining traditional methods of teaching; to impart quality education through state-of-the-art infrastructure; to practice and promote high standards of professional ethics, transparency and accountability.

q: What accreditations does NNRG have?
a: NAAC accreditation (A+ Grade), NBA accreditation (CSE, ECE, ME), UGC recognition under section 2(f), ISO 9001:2015 certified.

q: Who is the Chairman of NNRG?
a: Sri Nalla Narasimha Reddy is the Chairman and founding member of NNRG.

q: What are NNRG's core values?
a: Social Justice, Equity, Innovation, Creativity, Entrepreneurial Approach, Socio-Economic Values, Culture, Environmental Consciousness, State-of-the-art Technology, Quality.

q: What is NNRG's motto?
a: Dream Big and Aim High.

q: Where is NNRG located?
a: NNRG is located at Chowdariguda Village, Ghatkesar Mandal, Ranga Reddy District, Telangana - 500088, approximately 17 km from Secunderabad.

q: How many engineering departments does NNRG have?
a: NNRG has 5 engineering departments: Computer Science & Engineering (CSE), Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE), Electrical & Electronics Engineering (EEE), Mechanical Engineering (ME), and Civil Engineering.

q: What is the CSE intake at NNRG?
a: CSE has an intake of 360 seats in the B.Tech program.

q: What is the ECE intake?
a: ECE has an intake of 180 seats for B.Tech and 18 seats for M.Tech VLSI & Embedded Systems.

q: What is the mechanical engineering intake?
a: Mechanical Engineering has an intake of 120 seats for B.Tech and 18 seats for M.Tech.

q: What is the civil engineering intake?
a: Civil Engineering has an intake of 30 seats for B.Tech.

q: Does NNRG offer EEE?
a: Yes, NNRG offers Electrical & Electronics Engineering (EEE) as part of the School of Engineering.

q: What pharmacy programs does NNRG offer?
a: NNRG offers B.Pharmacy (4-year program) and M.Pharmacy specializations in Pharmaceutics and Pharmaceutical Analysis.

q: What is the intake for B.Pharmacy?
a: B.Pharmacy intake is listed; see school of pharmacy pages for exact numbers.

q: What MBA specializations are available?
a: MBA specializations in Marketing, Finance, and Human Resources (2-year program).

q: What is the MBA intake?
a: MBA intake is 60 seats.

q: Does NNRG offer M.Tech?
a: Yes, NNRG offers M.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering, VLSI & Embedded Systems, and Mechanical Engineering.

q: When was CSE department established?
a: CSE department was established in 2009.

q: Is CSE accredited by NBA?
a: Yes, CSE was accredited by NBA in 2018 and further accredited in 2022.

q: How many computer labs does CSE have?
a: CSE has seven computer labs with over 400 high-end systems.

q: What is the CSE student association?
a: Tech Samuuhah is the informal association of CSE students and faculty that conducts technical events.

q: When was ECE established?
a: ECE was established in 2009.

q: Is ECE accredited?
a: Yes, ECE was accredited by NBA in 2018.

q: What are ECE's focus areas?
a: Microelectronics, mobile communications, and VLSI design automation.

q: What is the ECE head of department?
a: Dr. Ravi Bolimera is the Head of ECE Department.

q: When was Mechanical Engineering department established?
a: Mechanical Engineering was established in 2009.

q: What CAD software is available in ME labs?
a: Pro-E, AutoCAD, and ANSYS simulation software are available.

q: Does ME have CNC machines?
a: Yes, the department has CNC machines for practical training.

q: When was Civil Engineering established?
a: Civil Engineering was established in 2010.

q: Who is the head of Civil department?
a: Dr. G. Subba Rao is the head of Civil Engineering.

q: Who is the head of the Data Science department?
a: Mrs V. Indrani is the HOD of the Data Science (DS) department at NNRG.

q: Who is the head of the AI & ML department?
a: Dr. G. Sravan Kumar is the HOD of the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AI & ML) department at NNRG.

q: Who is the head of DS?
a: Mrs V. Indrani is the HOD of the Data Science (DS) department at NNRG.

q: Who is the head of AI & ML?
a: Dr. G. Sravan Kumar is the HOD of the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AI & ML) department at NNRG.

q: What are civil engineering specializations at NNRG?
a: Structural engineering, water resources engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation engineering, and engineering geology.

q: How do I apply to NNRG?
a: Apply through JNTU state counseling, EAMCET, or through Management (Category-B) online application at https://nnrg.edu.in/bcategory/index.php.

q: What is management quota admission?
a: Management (Category-B/NRI/NRI Sponsored) seats are available for B.Tech and B.Pharmacy. Apply online at the bcategory portal.

q: What is the admissions contact number?
What is the admissions email?
a: adminssions@nnrg.edu.in

q: What are the eligibility criteria for B.Tech?
a: Passed Intermediate (10+2) or equivalent; EAMCET/JEE rank and/or management quota eligibility. See First Year Handbook for details.

q: What is the admission process?
a: Students apply through state counseling (JNTUH) or management portal; document verification and fee payment follow; see admission pages for checklist.

q: How many books does NNRG library have?
a: The library has more than 30,000 volumes covering about 5,000 titles.

q: How many online journals are available?
a: The library provides access to approximately 600 online e-journals through DELNET.

q: How many print journals are available?
a: The library subscribes to 228 print journals.

q: What are the library timings?
a: 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM on all working days.

q: Can UG students borrow books?
a: Yes, UG students can borrow 3 books for 15 days with a late fee of ₹1 per day.

q: Can PG students borrow books?
a: Yes, PG students can borrow 3 books for 15 days with a late fee of ₹1 per day.

q: How many books can faculty borrow?
a: Teaching staff can borrow 4 books for one month.

q: What is the late fee for library books?
a: ₹1 per day for UG and PG students.

q: Does NNRG have a digital library?
a: Yes, the digital library has 30+ systems with 10 Mbps internet connection and access to NPTEL videos and e-journals.

q: Who is the NNRG librarian?
a: K. Srinivas is the Librarian at NNRG.

q: How many bus routes does NNRG have?
a: NNRG operates 12 bus routes covering various parts of the city.

q: What is the transportation fee?
a: Transportation fees range from ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 depending on the distance.

q: What time does Route 1 bus arrive at NNRG?
a: Route 1 bus arrives at NNRG at 8:45 AM.

q: Who is the driver for Route 1?
a: Narendra Kumar is the driver for Route 1 (Phone: 93909 43533).

q: Does NNRG provide RTC buses?
a: Yes, RTC city buses serve nearby areas like Ghatkesar, Korremula, and Narapally.

q: What is the campus distance from Secunderabad?
a: The campus is approximately 17 km from Secunderabad and 10 km from Uppal Ring Road.

q: Are there hostel facilities for girls?
a: Yes, NNRG has a girls hostel with various facilities.

q: Who is the girls hostel warden?
a: A. Vijaya Laxmi is the Girls Hostel Warden.

q: What facilities are available in girls hostel?
a: 24/7 medical care, indoor & outdoor games, entertainment hall with TV, visitors lounge, 24/7 security, RO water, solar heaters, and ragging-free environment.

q: Is the hostel ragging-free?
a: Yes, NNRG hostel is a ragging-free campus.

q: Does the hostel have medical facilities?
a: Yes, 24/7 medical care assistance is available with a dedicated vehicle for hospital transfers.

q: What is the placement percentage at NNRG?
a: The exact placement percentage varies by year; see placement statistics PDFs for year-wise data.

q: What companies visit NNRG for recruitment?
a: TCS, Infosys, ICICI Bank, Divi's Labs, Sutherland, PIE INFOCOMM, BYJU'S, Genpact, HETERO, Wipro, and many others.

q: What is the highest package offered at NNRG?
a: See placement cell reports for current highest package; recent announcements mention packages like 7.0 LPA at BYJU'S.

q: What is the average package at NNRG?
a: Average packages vary by department and batch; check placement statistics for year-wise averages.

q: Does NNRG conduct placement training?
a: Yes, Campus Recruitment Training (CRT) is conducted; placement cell also organizes training programs.

q: What internship programs are available?
a: NNRG collaborates with IBM, Infosys, ICT Academy for internships and skill development programs.

q: Who are the placement officers at NNRG?
a: Department-wise placement officers are listed on placement pages; contact admissions for specific contacts.

q: What is the library floor area?
a: The library is spread over a floor area of approximately 1,400 sqm.

q: What sports facilities does NNRG have?
a: Basketball, Cricket, Volleyball, Throw Ball, Football and indoor/outdoor games facilities.

q: Is there a cafeteria on campus?
a: Yes, a multi-cuisine, hygienic campus cafeteria is available with a campus store called 'READERS'.

q: What is available in the campus store?
a: The store 'READERS' offers stationery, gift articles, Xerox services, and energy drinks.

q: Does NNRG have Wi-Fi?
a: Yes, NNRG has campus-wide fully Wi-Fi network with 600 Mbps bandwidth.

q: What labs does NNRG have?
a: NNRG has state-of-the-art labs in each department including computer labs, CAD labs, simulation labs, and specialized labs.

q: Does NNRG have an auditorium?
a: Yes, NNRG has auditorium and seminar halls for events and presentations.

q: Is there a robotics center?
a: Yes, NNRG has a Robotics Development Centre for practical learning.

q: Does NNRG have an incubation center?
a: Yes, NNRG has an Incubation Center for student startup projects.

q: How many faculty members does NNRG have?
a: Total faculty count not specified; see department pages for individual faculty lists.

q: What is the qualification of NNRG faculty?
a: Faculty have PhDs, MTechs, and BTechs with 4+ years teaching experience minimum in senior positions.

q: Does NNRG conduct technical symposiums?
a: Yes; Tech Samprathi (CSE), Pharmasamprathi (Pharmacy), and department-specific symposia are regularly conducted.

q: What is PARAMPARA?
a: PARAMPARA is a National Level Management Fest organized by the School of Management Sciences.

q: Does NNRG have cultural events?
a: Yes, including MUN (Model United Nations), hackathons, workshops, and cultural activities.

q: Are there research and development activities?
a: Yes, R&D cell and innovation center support faculty and student research projects.

q: What is the ERP system URL?
a: https://exams-nnrg.in/

q: Where can I check exam results?
a: Results are declared through notifications on the latest events page and via the ERP system.

q: How do I apply for re-evaluation of exams?
a: Applications for revaluation/recounting are submitted through the COE office; see exam notifications for deadlines.

q: What is the main campus address?
a: Chowdariguda Village, Ghatkesar Mandal, Ranga Reddy District, Telangana - 500088

q: What is the NNRG phone number?
a: 040 29705282 (Admissions); see contact page for department-specific numbers.

q: What is the NNRG email?
a: adminssions@nnrg.edu.in (Admissions); department emails available on department pages.

q: Does NNRG have a webmail?
a: Yes, webmail is available at https://webmail1.hostinger.in/

q: What is NNRG's ranking?
a: NNRG participates in NIRF rankings; see NIRF page on the website for latest rankings.

q: Is NNRG autonomous?
a: NNRG was conferred autonomous status for 10 years by UGC as announced in November 2021.

q: What is the campus size?
a: NNRG has an integrated multi-disciplinary campus; exact acreage not specified on the main pages.

q: How many students are enrolled at NNRG?
a: Exact enrollment numbers vary by year; total capacity across all programs is several thousands.

q: What is NNRG's industry collaboration approach?
a: Industry Institute Interaction Cell (IIIC) facilitates collaborations for internships, workshops, and recruitment.

q: Are there student clubs at NNRG?
a: Yes, including technical clubs, cultural clubs, NSS, NCC, and professional societies like IEEE, CSI, ISTE.

q: Does NNRG have an innovation council?
a: Yes, Institution's Innovation Council (IIC) organizes hackathons and innovation challenges.

q: What is the NNRG scholarship policy?
a: NNRG offers scholarships and fee concessions; see admissions page for details.

q: Does NNRG have grievance redressal system?
a: Yes, NNRG has grievance cells and anti-ragging mechanisms as per UGC norms.

q: Is there women protection cell?
a: Yes, NNRG has women protection and safety cells.

q: What is IQAC at NNRG?
a: IQAC (Internal Quality Assurance Cell) oversees quality improvement and accreditation processes.

q: What is NAAC accreditation?
a: NNRG is accredited by NAAC (National Assessment and Accreditation Council) with A+ Grade.

q: What is NBA accreditation?
a: Programs in CSE, ECE, and ME are accredited by NBA (National Board of Accreditation).
